mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 100+ rate cut observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above 0.5pp, or the signal fails to predict direction in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day returns in over 60% of historical quarters, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during high-inflation regimes signal supply constraints that erode corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high inflation periods yield positive 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL falls below 300.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 4.0% combined with rapid tightening expectations triggers immediate liquidity withdrawal, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment alongside falling unemployment rates confirms broad-based labor market strength that supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 55% of historical months, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts expansion during periods of low interest rates signals strong residential investment that boosts economic activity and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases during low-rate periods do not lead to positive 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the predictive relationship reverses when rates exceed 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly industrial production growth above 0.5% signals accelerating manufacturing output that improves near-term corporate earnings expectations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of months where INDPRO grows faster than 0.5%, or the signal shows no statistical edge in walk-forward validation.
